description.en:
Unified VOICES ontology for Holocaust survivor testimony knowledge graphs.
    Thesaurus-free public variant. External standards: CIDOC-CRM, OWL-Time,
    MFOEM, Web Annotation, PROV-O, Media Ontology.

comments.en:
Unified VOICES ontology for Holocaust survivor testimony knowledge graphs.
    Thesaurus-free public variant. External standards: CIDOC-CRM, OWL-Time,
    MFOEM, Web Annotation, PROV-O, Media Ontology.

publisher.value: Luxembourg Institute of Science and Technology (LIST)

comment.en: Manner or mechanism of an event (by train, on foot, etc.).
A life or historical episode described by the survivor, linked to participants, place, time, and emotions.
Reason or circumstance that led to the event.
An annotation associating a narrated event with an MFOEM emotion category and valence/arousal scores.
Type of action described (deportation, forced labor, escape, etc.).
A canonical historical event (e.g. Kristallnacht, Camp Liberation) for alignment.
A Web Annotation attaching an interpretive body to a narrated event or interview segment.
A recorded oral testimony session grouped into several segments.
A time-bounded transcript fragment describing events, entities, and feelings.
An observable physiological expression during narration, such as crying, silence, or laughter, detected from transcriber annotations.
A human participant involved in the testimony or event.
Numeric embedding representing semantic vectors for events.
Calibrated valence score of an emotion annotation.
Confidence score of an outward authority link.
Identifier of the community assigned by community detection.
Cosine-similarity score of a similarTo relation.
The raw text expression from which temporal information was parsed.
Start time of a segment in milliseconds from recording start.
Title of the testimony.
Identifier of the source utterance of a segment.
Confidence score for an entity match or alignment.
Calibrated arousal score of an emotion annotation.
Numeric identifier of the interview.
Short textual summary of the interview.
Name of the model used to compute an embedding vector.
Year the interview was recorded.
URL of a thumbnail image for the media resource.
Step in the extraction pipeline that produced this annotation.
Precision level of a temporal annotation (month, year, year_range, relative, unknown).
Algorithm used for community detection (e.g. louvain).
Owning collection or archive of an interview.
Raw free-text temporal reference extracted for a narrated event.
Raw free-text activity description extracted for a narrated event.
Links an interview segment to a temporal clip of the audiovisual recording.
URL of the audiovisual media resource.
Verbatim transcript text of a segment (not redistributed publicly).
Recorded gender of a person, where available.
Confidence of a temporal parse result.
Method used to produce the annotation (regex, llm_extraction, etc.).
Graded intensity of the observed physiological process.
Name of the model or algorithm used to compute a result.
Links an interview to the audiovisual recording representing it.
Method used to match or link an entity (e.g. exact_label, local_mint, external_alignment).
Confidence score for the annotated statement (0.0-1.0).
Links an interview segment to an observed physiological process (e.g. crying).
Historical-period bucket assigned to a narrated event's time interval.
Ordinal position of a segment within its interview.
Dimensionality of an embedding vector.
Intensity score of an emotion or physiological annotation.
Name of the physiological process (e.g. crying, silence, laughter).
End time of a segment in milliseconds from recording start.
The regex pattern that triggered a classification.
Controlled emotion category label of an emotion annotation.
Speaker label of a segment (e.g. interviewer vs interviewee).
